title: QA foundation: tester space, process, and full testing pyramid (FE + BE)
summary: Stand up a dedicated QA workspace + canonical testing pyramid (unit/integration/e2e/visual) shared by frontend and backend.
problem: Today there is no QA hub, no agreed-on test layer ratios, and no single page that explains the difference between unit, integration, and e2e tests for this codebase. Coverage looks healthy but levels overlap and no one owns the gaps.
actions: Create internal/qa/{overview,layers,tooling,escalation}.html
Document the pyramid: target ratios per layer + when to add a test at each level
Pick canonical tooling per layer (pytest, Playwright, Hypothesis, k6) and pin versions
Wire QA hub into nav-tree-internal.json + cross-link from roles/qa
Add a roles/qa/radar entry per layer so blind spots are visible
acceptance: QA overview page renders and shows the full pyramid with current coverage per layer
Each layer has its own how-to page with a runnable example
Sidebar lists the QA hub; roles/qa cross-links to it

title: Integration tests against the database (CI)
summary: Run integration tests against a real database in CI, not a mock — catch migration + concurrency regressions before merge.
problem: Current backend tests stub the DB layer. Mock/prod divergence has bitten us; migrations pass locally but break on a fresh schema. We need a CI job that boots a throwaway Postgres and runs the full integration suite against it.
actions: Add a Postgres service container to .github/workflows/test.yml
Bootstrap schema via the same migration command used in prod
Mark integration tests with a pytest marker so they can run separately
Add a `make test-integration` target that mirrors CI locally
Document the test-data seeding contract
acceptance: CI job `test-integration` runs against Postgres on every PR
Migration regressions surface in CI (verified via a known-bad seed)
Local `make test-integration` matches CI behaviour

title: Working Swagger for frontend validation only
summary: Mount Swagger UI in the portal so frontend devs can validate request/response shapes without spinning up the backend.
problem: Frontend devs need a way to see live OpenAPI examples without running the backend. The current spec is static markdown; Swagger UI never finishes loading because of asset paths.
actions: Mount Swagger UI under /reference/api/swagger with the canonical openapi.yaml as source
Pin the UI version and host the bundle locally (no CDN)
Hide write operations — frontend validation only, no `Try it out`
Add a portal nav entry under Reference > API
acceptance: Swagger UI loads with all endpoints and example payloads
No console errors; assets served from same origin
Reference > API > Swagger link works from the sidebar

title: Requirements: traceability, stakeholder map, requirement conflicts
summary: Build a traceability matrix that ties every requirement to its stakeholder, source document, and test — and flag where requirements contradict.
problem: Requirements live scattered across RFCs, ADRs, and conversation threads. There is no single map showing who asked for what, where the requirement lives, or whether two stakeholders want incompatible things.
actions: Author internal/sa/requirements/traceability.html with a JSON-backed matrix
Add a stakeholder map page (org chart with goals + concerns per actor)
Document the conflict-resolution protocol (RACI + escalation path)
Cross-link each ADR to the requirement(s) it satisfies
acceptance: Matrix renders with at least 15 real requirements traced end-to-end
Every active RFC links back to its stakeholder + source requirement
Conflict page lists at least 3 documented trade-offs with resolutions

title: Read-model caching strategy and fallback policy
summary: Define the caching strategy for read models — TTLs, invalidation triggers, and what to serve when the cache is cold or stale.
problem: Read paths re-query the primary every time; latency is fine today but won't be at 10× load. There's no documented policy for when to cache, when to invalidate, or what to return when the cache is unavailable.
actions: Write an ADR covering: caching tiers, TTL per read model, invalidation hooks
Define the fallback policy: serve-stale vs hard-fail per endpoint
Add a `cache-policy.yaml` SoT consumed by the runtime
Add metrics: cache hit ratio, stale-serve count, fallback count
acceptance: ADR merged with stakeholder sign-off
Top 3 read endpoints honour the cache-policy.yaml at runtime
Dashboard shows hit ratio per endpoint

title: Async REST pattern: 202 + job status + pagination/sorting
summary: Codify the async-REST pattern: 202 Accepted + job-status endpoint + canonical pagination/sorting query params.
problem: Long-running endpoints currently block; some return 200 immediately and lie, some hang. Pagination/sorting params are ad-hoc per endpoint. Frontend can't predict how any new endpoint behaves.
actions: Write an ADR fixing the 202 → /jobs/{id} → 200 contract
Standardise pagination (`?page`, `?per_page`, `Link` header)
Standardise sorting (`?sort=field,-field2`)
Add OpenAPI examples for each pattern; update existing endpoints incrementally
acceptance: ADR merged
At least 2 long-running endpoints follow the new contract end-to-end
OpenAPI lint rule enforces pagination/sort param naming

title: DB capacity + SQL performance playbook + index audit
summary: Capture a database capacity model + SQL-perf playbook + index audit so query regressions don't ship silently.
problem: We have no model for `rows × access frequency × QPS` at expected growth, no runbook for diagnosing slow queries, and indexes were added reactively. The first scaling event will hurt.
actions: Build a capacity model (table + sparkline) for top-10 tables
Write internal/sre/db-perf-playbook.html: EXPLAIN walkthrough, common pitfalls
Run an index audit; document each non-PK index with the query that justifies it
Add a periodic `pg_stat_statements` snapshot job + dashboard
acceptance: Capacity model published with refresh recipe
Playbook has 3 worked examples (slow query → fixed)
Index audit doc lists every index + its justification

title: Concurrency scenarios: ACID/isolation guide + race tests
summary: Document the concurrency model (ACID + isolation level + locking) and back it with race-condition tests for the top 3 write paths.
problem: Isolation level isn't documented; nobody knows whether the system tolerates phantom reads. There are zero tests that intentionally interleave writes to verify safety. Race bugs will be hard to catch in prod.
actions: Write internal/architect/concurrency.html: ACID, isolation levels, our choices
Identify top 3 write paths most exposed to concurrency
Add race tests (pytest-asyncio gather pattern) for each
Document the locking primitives used (row locks, advisory locks)
acceptance: Concurrency guide merged
3 race tests in CI; deliberately failing variant proves they're real
Each top-3 write path has its lock strategy documented inline

title: Security hardening: PII classification, masking, retention
summary: Classify every PII field, define masking rules for logs/exports, and set retention windows per data class.
problem: PII isn't classified; logs may leak emails; we have no documented retention policy. Compliance review would fail today.
actions: Inventory PII fields across the schema; tag each (`pii.email`, `pii.phone`, …)
Add a masking layer for log output (structured logging hook)
Define retention windows per class; document in internal/sre/data-retention.html
Add a yearly purge job spec
acceptance: Field-level PII tags exist in the schema metadata
Logs sampled in CI contain no raw PII
Retention policy page published with sign-off block

title: Playwright visual-regression harness for component galleries
summary: Wire a Playwright visual-diff job over the UI Kit showcase pages so accidental visual regressions block merges.
problem: UI Kit changes ripple across 100+ pages. Today regressions are caught manually in the browser — usually after merge. We need pixel-diff coverage on the canonical showcase URLs.
actions: Add Playwright + visual-comparison dep with pinned versions
Identify the 20 most-load-bearing showcase pages
Take baseline snapshots in light + dark + mobile viewports
Add CI job that fails on > 0.1% diff with annotated artefacts
acceptance: `make visual-test` runs locally and produces diffs
CI fails on a deliberate visual regression
Baselines stored under tests/visual/baselines/ with refresh recipe

title: Public/internal documentation portal IA + governance consolidation (RFC 0004)
summary: Land RFC 0004: a single, audited IA for public + internal portals; consolidate governance pages under one nav root.
problem: Pages drifted into 4 parallel taxonomies (governance/, sa/, internal/, public/). Same content lives in 2-3 places; sidebars contradict each other. RFC 0004 is mid-flight (phases E + F left).
actions: Finish phase E: collapse governance/ into internal/governance/
Finish phase F: emit the consolidated nav-tree-internal.json from a generator
Verify with check_asset_refs.py — zero stale hrefs
Update sidebars for both portals to the new IA
Close out RFC 0004 with status=accepted + audit notes
acceptance: RFC 0004 marked accepted
Sidebar matches the canonical IA on both portals
check_asset_refs.py exits 0

title: Test pyramid: unit tests + property-based tests (Hypothesis)
summary: Backfill unit tests + add Hypothesis property tests for the pure-function layer (validators, parsers, scoring).
problem: Unit coverage is lopsided — service layer at 80%, pure utils at 30%. Pure functions are exactly where property-based tests pay off; we don't run any today.
actions: Identify pure-function modules (validators, parsers, scoring)
Add Hypothesis dep + write strategies per input type
Author property tests for the top 5 functions
Document the Hypothesis pattern in qa/layers/property-based.html
acceptance: Unit coverage on pure-function modules ≥ 90%
At least 5 property tests in CI
Pattern doc has a runnable example

title: E2E: HTTP scenarios against a running application
summary: Add HTTP-level e2e scenarios that hit a real running app (compose stack) covering the top 5 user journeys.
problem: Our highest-value tests today stop at the service layer. Nothing exercises the actual HTTP boundary including middleware, auth, and serialisation. Regressions in middleware are invisible.
actions: Add `make e2e-up` that boots the compose stack with seeded data
Pick 5 user journeys (login → action → result)
Write each as an HTTP-level scenario using httpx
Add CI job that runs e2e against the stack
acceptance: 5 e2e scenarios pass locally and in CI
Failure surfaces the offending HTTP step, not a stack trace
Seed data is reproducible across runs

title: Mutation testing pilot for critical rules
summary: Pilot mutation testing (mutmut) on the scoring + validation rules to measure how often tests would catch a real bug.
problem: Coverage % overstates safety — branches can be 100% covered by tests that assert nothing meaningful. Mutation testing exposes weak assertions.
actions: Add mutmut dev dependency
Run against scoring + validation modules only (scoped pilot)
Document mutation score; convert top survivors into test gaps
Decide: roll out or shelve based on signal/cost
acceptance: Pilot report with mutation score per module
At least 3 test gaps converted into new tests
Decision doc (adopt/shelve) merged

title: Distributed tracing (OpenTelemetry) and trace context
summary: Instrument services with OpenTelemetry so a request can be followed end-to-end across the HTTP → DB → cache hops.
problem: Debugging latency outliers means grepping logs across 3 services and guessing causality. Trace IDs aren't propagated. SRE work is way harder than it needs to be.
actions: Add opentelemetry-sdk + auto-instrumentation for FastAPI + SQLAlchemy
Propagate trace context via W3C traceparent header
Ship traces to a local Jaeger in compose; document setup
Add the trace ID to log output for correlation
acceptance: A single request shows a complete trace tree in Jaeger
Log lines carry the trace ID
SRE runbook page documents how to read a trace

title: Single source of truth for application / OpenAPI version metadata
summary: Pick one source of truth for version metadata (app version, OpenAPI version, image tag) and derive every other surface from it.
problem: Version drifts across pyproject.toml, openapi.yaml, the Docker tag, and the portal footer. Releases ship with mismatched numbers.
actions: Pick pyproject.toml as the canonical version source
Generate openapi.yaml `info.version` from it at build time
Stamp the Docker tag + portal footer from the same source
Add a pre-commit check that fails on drift
acceptance: All four surfaces show the same version at all times
Pre-commit fails if any drifts
Release runbook references the single source

title: CI: optional HTTP smoke or load-regression job
summary: Add an opt-in CI job that runs a 60-second HTTP smoke / load regression so latency regressions surface before release.
problem: Performance regressions are only caught when someone notices the UI feel slow. By then they're already in main. A short k6 burst on demand would prevent that.
actions: Author a small k6 script: 100 RPS for 60s against the top endpoints
Wire it as a manual-dispatch GH workflow
Publish a baseline; fail if p95 regresses > 25%
Document how to interpret the report
acceptance: Manual-dispatch workflow exists and runs to completion
Baseline checked into the repo
A deliberate slowdown causes the job to fail

title: Redis adoption: idempotency cache and short-lived platform data
summary: Introduce Redis for two specific use cases: idempotency keys and short-lived platform data (sessions, rate limits).
problem: We currently fake idempotency in Postgres which costs writes. Sessions also sit in Postgres. Both are textbook Redis use cases. Adopting it now keeps the schema clean later.
actions: Add Redis to compose + production manifest
Build an idempotency middleware backed by Redis SETNX + TTL
Migrate session store to Redis with a feature flag
Document Redis ops: persistence, eviction, backup
acceptance: Idempotency middleware live with a passing e2e test
Session store flag flips with zero downtime
Ops doc covers eviction + failover

title: Document versioning: Document Version field and change policy
summary: Add a `Document Version` field + change policy to every governance doc so reviewers know which revision they signed off on.
problem: Docs get edited in place without a version stamp; sign-off applies to 'whatever the page said at the time'. Hard to audit later.
actions: Define the policy: bump major on breaking, minor on additive
Add a docs-meta block to the canonical page template
Backfill the field on the top 20 governance pages
Add a pre-commit check that warns when a page changes without a bump
acceptance: Policy page merged
Top 20 governance pages carry a version stamp
Pre-commit warns on un-bumped edits

title: PDoc: navigation and unified application theme
summary: Theme the pdoc-generated API reference to match the portal, with a working sidebar that mirrors the module tree.
problem: pdoc output sits at /reference/api/python/ with default styling; the nav is broken and it looks like a different product. Users bounce.
actions: Author a pdoc HTML template that loads the portal stylesheet
Wire pdoc's nav into the portal sidebar at build time
Verify dark mode + mobile layout still work
Add a make target so the docs rebuild deterministically
acceptance: Generated reference looks visually identical to a hand-authored portal page
Sidebar nav navigates between modules
Dark mode + mobile tested

title: Modeling pack: UML Activity + State Machine + BPMN + RACI
summary: Ship a 'modeling pack' page: UML Activity, State Machine, BPMN, and RACI templates with the conventions we use.
problem: Different docs use different notations for the same things; readers waste time decoding. A canonical pack settles the question.
actions: Author internal/sa/modeling/pack.html with one canonical example per notation
Add PlantUML source for each example (no Mermaid)
Document when to reach for each notation
Cross-link from architect onboarding
acceptance: Modeling pack page published with 4 worked examples
Examples render via Kroki
Architect onboarding links to the pack

title: Estimation governance: plan/fact, forecasting, stakeholder SLA
summary: Document the estimation model: plan-vs-fact tracking, forecasting rules, and the SLA we commit to stakeholders.
problem: Estimates are guesses logged nowhere. There's no plan-vs-fact loop, so the next estimate is no better than the last. Stakeholders don't know what 'P1 in W21' actually promises.
actions: Author internal/manager/estimation/overview.html
Add a plan/fact table to each retrospective
Document the forecasting heuristic (rolling 4-sprint median)
Publish the stakeholder SLA matrix (P0..P3 → response time)
acceptance: Estimation overview page merged
Last 4 sprints have a plan/fact entry
SLA matrix is referenced from the public-facing roadmap

title: Feature flags and A/B experimentation baseline
summary: Pick a feature-flag system + write the experimentation baseline (hypothesis → metric → readout) we expect every A/B to follow.
problem: Risky changes either ship behind a manual env var or not at all. No team-wide pattern for A/B. The first real experiment will reinvent the wheel.
actions: Evaluate GrowthBook vs Unleash vs a homegrown YAML flag file
Pick one and document the integration
Write the experimentation template: hypothesis → metric → readout
Run one toy A/B to validate end-to-end
acceptance: ADR records the choice
Library wired into one endpoint with a flag
Toy A/B has a published readout

title: Interactive component explorer (Storybook-tier state previews)
summary: Add an interactive explorer to each UI Kit component page: pick variant + state + theme + viewport and see it live.
problem: Showcase pages render one canonical example. Reviewers can't see 'how does this button look in dark mode, disabled, on mobile?' without writing markup.
actions: Design a `<component-explorer>` web component (variant/state/theme/viewport selectors)
Mount it on the top 10 component pages
Persist last-used state in localStorage per component
Document the contract (data-* attrs for variant axes)
acceptance: Explorer renders on 10 component pages
All four axes toggle independently and update the preview
State persists across reloads

title: Backfill screen specs for top-3 unspec'd pages
summary: Write screen specs (purpose, states, copy, data deps, a11y) for the 3 most-trafficked pages that currently lack one.
problem: Some flagship pages have zero spec. Any redesign starts from reverse-engineering the live page — slow, error-prone, and erases the original intent.
actions: Identify the 3 unspec'd pages by traffic
For each: write purpose, states, copy, data deps, a11y notes, error cases
Cross-link to component explorer entries
Add to internal/sa/screens/ index
acceptance: 3 screen-spec pages published
Each includes wireframe + state table
screens/ index sorted by traffic

title: Per-component testing matrix (link spec to e2e/unit tests)
summary: Add a 'Testing' section to every UI Kit component page that links to the unit + e2e + visual tests covering it.
problem: Spec, code, and tests live in 3 places. Component reviewers can't tell what's actually tested without grepping. Coverage gaps are invisible.
actions: Add a `tests.json` per component listing covering test files
Render a 'Testing' section in the showcase template
Backfill the json for the top 20 components
Add a check that warns when a component has no tests linked
acceptance: Top 20 components show a populated Testing section
Check warns on components without test links
Section links resolve in CI

title: Changelog rollup automation from page-history tables
summary: Auto-generate a weekly changelog rollup by scraping every page's docs-history block.
problem: Each page has its own history table, but there's no portal-wide view of what changed this week. Stakeholders ask 'what's new?' and the answer is 'go look around'.
actions: Write a scraper that walks all *.html and extracts docs-history rows
Group by week; render to internal/governance/changelog.html
Add an RSS feed
Cron weekly via GitHub Actions
acceptance: Changelog page lists last 8 weeks of changes
RSS validates
Workflow runs Monday 09:00

title: Render review_slot_state_machine.svg (blocked on Kroki)
summary: Re-render review_slot_state_machine.svg — currently a broken image because Kroki rejects the embedded PlantUML.
problem: The diagram source uses a PlantUML feature Kroki's pinned version doesn't recognise, so the SVG ships as a broken image on every page referencing it.
actions: Reproduce the Kroki rejection locally
Either upgrade Kroki or rewrite the diagram with supported syntax
Re-render to SVG; commit
Add a render check to pre-commit so this can't regress
acceptance: SVG renders in the portal
Pre-commit catches a deliberate syntax break
Issue linked to the fixed commit

title: RabbitMQ integration — async inter-service messaging
summary: Stand up RabbitMQ for async inter-service messaging — publish/consume contract, DLQ, and ops runbook.
problem: Inter-service calls are HTTP-synchronous today. Long-running flows block the caller; failures cascade. A broker decouples them but we have no chosen tech, no contract, no runbook.
actions: Add RabbitMQ to compose + production manifest
Define the message contract (envelope, headers, idempotency key)
Implement a publisher + consumer for one real flow
Document DLQ behaviour and the SRE runbook
acceptance: One real flow runs through RabbitMQ end-to-end
DLQ exists and is monitored
Runbook covers: stuck consumer, full queue, broker down

title: Distributed rate limiting with Redis (multi-instance)
summary: Replace per-instance in-memory rate limiting with a Redis-backed shared counter so limits actually hold under multi-instance deploys.
problem: Rate limit lives in process memory; with 3 instances a client gets 3× the limit. A misbehaving caller can still take us down.
actions: Pick the algorithm: token bucket vs sliding window
Implement on Redis with Lua for atomicity
Replace the in-memory middleware
Add a chaos test: 3 instances + 1 abusive client
acceptance: Aggregate limit holds across instances under load
Chaos test passes
Per-route limits documented in the API reference

title: PostgreSQL as primary database and migration path from SQLite
summary: Make Postgres the primary; document and rehearse the SQLite → Postgres migration path so dev can stay on SQLite if they want.
problem: SQLite has carried us this far but blocks concurrency work, real index audits, and many ORM features. We need a clean cutover with a documented rollback.
actions: Add Postgres to compose; make it the default DSN
Migrate the test suite + CI to Postgres
Document the data export/import procedure
Rehearse the cutover on a staging snapshot
acceptance: CI runs against Postgres
Staging cutover rehearsal documented with timing
Rollback path tested

title: Test data: seeds, fixtures, runbook
summary: Build a canonical test-data set + fixtures + the runbook for refreshing it after a schema change.
problem: Every test file rolls its own fixtures. Schema changes break tests in surprising places because nobody owns the seed data.
actions: Pick a fixture pattern (factory-boy or hand-rolled SQL)
Author a canonical seed set covering all entity types
Document the refresh runbook for schema changes
Migrate at least 3 noisy test files to the shared fixtures
acceptance: Shared fixtures used by ≥ 3 test files
Seed runbook merged
A deliberate schema change updates fixtures in one place

title: Feature flags (safe rollout)
summary: Production-grade feature-flag plumbing (separate from the A/B baseline) — percentage rollouts, kill switches, audit log.
problem: BL-038 picks the tech. This task is the production plumbing: percentage rollouts, kill switches, audit log of who flipped what when.
actions: Wire the chosen flag system into the runtime
Add a percentage-rollout primitive
Add an audit log of flag changes
Document the on-call kill-switch procedure
acceptance: Flag flips are logged with actor + timestamp
Percentage rollout works in a smoke test
On-call procedure documented + tested

title: Architecture fitness: enforced layer boundaries
summary: Add architecture-fitness checks (import-linter) that fail CI when a layer imports across the boundary.
problem: We have a layered architecture in our heads. Nothing enforces it. Domain code already reaches into the HTTP layer in 2 places.
actions: Add import-linter with rules per layer
Fix existing violations
Wire into pre-commit + CI
Document the layer contract
acceptance: import-linter passes on main
Deliberate violation fails CI
Layer contract page merged

title: CI: container image SBOM and vulnerability scan
summary: Emit an SBOM per container image and fail CI on HIGH/CRITICAL vulns.
problem: We ship containers without an SBOM and without a vuln scan. A known-CVE base image could ship to prod today and we wouldn't know.
actions: Add syft to produce the SBOM
Add grype/trivy to scan; fail on HIGH+
Attach SBOM as a release artefact
Document the override path for false positives
acceptance: Every release has an SBOM
Vuln scan blocks merge on HIGH/CRITICAL
Override path tested

title: Deprecation framework and migration guide template
summary: Adopt a deprecation framework: lifecycle stages, deprecation notice format, migration-guide template.
problem: Deprecations are announced inconsistently — sometimes in a release note, sometimes nowhere. Consumers find out by breakage. We need a predictable shape.
actions: Define lifecycle stages (active/deprecated/sunset/removed)
Author the migration-guide template
Add a docs-meta `deprecation` block
Backfill on 3 already-deprecated endpoints
acceptance: Framework page merged

Template used for the next real migration

title: Component dependency graph (auto-generated)

title: Motion specs with frame-by-frame video / Lottie reference
summary: Add motion specs to every animated component: easing curve, duration, frame-by-frame reference or Lottie file.
problem: Animation curves and durations live in CSS but nowhere in the spec; reviewers can't say 'this feels off' with specifics. Cross-team handoff is verbal.

title: CI pipeline: quality gates, coverage, docs-check
summary: CI pipeline with quality gates: ruff, mypy, pytest coverage threshold, docs link-check.

Add docs-check (check_asset_refs.py)
Cache deps for speed

title: Repository cleanup + dead code removal
summary: Removed dead modules, unused dependencies, stale dirs. Repo footprint down 18%.
problem: Repo accumulated orphan files from earlier experiments; navigating got harder every month.
actions: Run vulture for dead-code candidates
Audit deps with pip-tools' unused detector
Delete agreed-on files in batches
Verify tests + docs still pass
acceptance: Repo size reduced
No tests removed without deliberate intent
Audit log entry per deletion batch

title: Structured logging (JSON) + correlation IDs
summary: Switched to structured JSON logging + per-request correlation ID propagated through middleware.
problem: Logs were unstructured strings; impossible to filter or correlate across services.
actions: Adopt structlog with JSON renderer
Generate correlation ID in middleware; pass via context
Include in every log call automatically
Update the SRE log query examples
acceptance: All log lines parse as JSON
Correlation ID present on every line of a request
Log query examples updated
